title: Make `make localunit` work on macOS
body:
With this,
```console
make binaries localunit BUILDTAGS='btrfs_noversion libdm_no_deferred_remove exclude_graphdriver_btrfs containers_image_ostree_stub varlink'
```
(i.e. what `make` does, except removing the `seccomp` tag) almost works; only the one more commit in https://github.com/mtrmac/libpod/tree/tests-WIP is necessary.

See the individual commit messages for more details.

RFC:
- Is `checkseccomp` used anywhere? I couldn’t find any reference except for the `Makefile`.
- Is there a clean way to do what https://github.com/mtrmac/libpod/tree/tests-WIP tries to? The tests need to be skipped because the default configuration uses `seccomp`, and that is entirely unrelated from the subject of the test. Both brute-force Linux checks and targeted checks against `seccomp` would work, but the tests could continue to be skipped even if the underlying implementation changes entirely and skipping them were no longer useful.

comments.body: Checkseccomp is not presently used, but we were planning to add a seccomp tag again, so I'd like to keep it for when we do.
I'm fine with a GOOS-based `Skip()` on tests that won't work on a certain architecture.
Also, it looks like `checkseccomp` is not what I thought it was - I think we still want to keep it, but we should not be building it anywhere but Linux.

I'm generally opposed to the BoltDB state test changes based on whether the command requires reading. We should not be passing states that do not support reading a container out of the database, so we're going to end up ripping these changes out once we implement that.

(Also, there's no reason why the entire `getContainerFromDB()` function is Linux-specific - maybe 2 lines of it actually require Linux...)

> I'm generally opposed to the BoltDB state test changes based on whether the command requires reading. We should not be passing states that do not support reading a container out of the database, so we're going to end up ripping these changes out once we implement that.

Sure, there’s nothing _inherent_ about reading states not working on macOS.  Would just skipping _all_ Bolt tests in `runForAllStates` on non-Linux, for now, work for you? Maybe gated with a platform-specific boolean constant again, to make sure if this is ever implemented, the tests are re-enabled globally.

> (Also, there's no reason why the entire getContainerFromDB() function is Linux-specific - maybe 2 lines of it actually require Linux...)

Yes, IIRC just this:
```go
		netNS, err := joinNetNS(nsPath)
		if err == nil {
			ctr.state.NetNS = netNS
```

`joinNetNS` ultimately calls `github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pkg/ns`, which has no Linux implementation.

The way `containerPlatformState` works, it can’t represent information for non-native platforms, so loading a Linux object on macOS would lose data. Changing that design to preserve everything, if it were desired, would be a larger project than I’m currently ready to take on.

OTOH if the design goal is that every platform can only read/write its own data, I can pretty trivially factor out the platform-specific parts out of `getContainerFromDB` (and yes, all existing unit tests pass when I modify the code to refuse to load anything with `netNSBytes != nil`!).

So, 3 options WRT `BoltState`:
- Disable tests on non-Linux.
- Decide to reject platform-incompatible containers, tests will pass everywhere.
- Decide to eventually support reading/writing platform-incompatible containers without losing data, and defer making that work.

@mtrmac Right now, BoltDB is explicitly set to error if the DB is opened on a machine with another GOOS - so I don't think we have to worry about Mac containers ending up on a Linux system, and vice-versa.

> Right now, BoltDB is explicitly set to error if the DB is opened on a machine with another GOOS - so I don't think we have to worry about Mac containers ending up on a Linux system, and vice-versa.

Ah, I didn’t notice that. Thanks!

Updated:
- Dropped the “test requires reading” conditions; kept only a few `s/assert/require/` to avoid panics in tests.
- Made `BoltState.getContainerFromDB` work on all platforms by factoring out the NetNS-specific code into a new function.
- Included the last commit from the `tests-WIP` branch in here as well; this skips some CLI tests based on `GOOS`. The value of working unit tests on macOS is worth a little ugly cross-layer knowledge in tests, I hope.

With this, all unit tests (with the command in the PR description) pass.  The only possibly remaining bit would be to teach `Makefile` to skip the `seccomp` tag on non-Linux.
LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+
